clinical_coherence.notes: 19F left-handed East Asian with temporal lobe seizures. The clinical presentation is consistent with right TLE (fear aura, behavioral arrest, left hand automatisms). Prolonged febrile seizure at age 2 (25 minutes) is a classic MTS risk factor. Family history of seizures is appropriate. However, PMH includes iron deficiency anemia diagnosed at age 22 and anxiety disorder diagnosed at age 24 - but the patient is only 19 years old. This is a clear temporal inconsistency from template error.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG, MRI, video-EEG, PET, and neuropsych findings are all concordant for right temporal lobe epilepsy with hippocampal sclerosis. Fear aura is appropriately linked to amygdala involvement. All tool outputs are internally consistent.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G40.109 correct. Differential is well-calibrated including panic disorder (important given fear aura). Critical actions and reasoning points are clinically accurate. The note about levetiracetam vs lamotrigine choice in a young woman of childbearing potential is clinically relevant.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic right TLE with MTS and concordant findings. Fear aura adds slight complexity in differentiating from panic disorder, but overall presentation is straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: Age inconsistency: patient is 19 years old but PMH includes diagnoses at age 22 and age 24. This is a template error where the age was changed but PMH was not updated.

educational_value.notes: Excellent teaching case for fear aura (amygdala localization), panic disorder differential, and AED considerations in young women of childbearing potential (teratogenicity risk).

issues_found: CRITICAL: Patient is 19 years old but PMH includes iron deficiency anemia diagnosed at age 22 and anxiety disorder at age 24 - temporal impossibility
Family history of paternal seizures in his twenties could warrant more detailed characterization
strengths: Fear aura as amygdala localization is a distinct teaching point
Panic disorder differential is clinically important with fear aura
AED teratogenicity considerations in young women are high-yield
